characters.description: Chicago's professional wizard, now wearing the gray cloak of a Warden of the White Council - a bitter irony for a man the Wardens once wanted to execute. The vampire war drags on, the Council is stretched to breaking, and Harry opens the book forced to witness the execution of a teenage warlock, the Council's justice at its most merciless. When word reaches him that black magic is moving in Chicago, the hunt leads somewhere that tests every loyalty he has - and what he does about it will put his own neck under the sword.
characters.name: Harry Dresden

characters.description: The most enigmatic member of the Senior Council, keeper of the Outer Gates. After the execution that opens the book, Rashid quietly points Harry at a problem: traces of black magic in Chicago that the Council's own resources have not run down. His hints are sparse and deliberate, as if he already knows more than he can say, and his trust in Harry - rare among the Council's leadership - carries its own weight of expectation.
characters.name: Rashid

characters.description: Harry's half-brother, the White Court vampire, still nominally sharing Harry's apartment but increasingly absent and evasive about where his money and time are going. Whatever he is hiding, his loyalty is not in question: when Harry mounts the most dangerous rescue of his life, Thomas is beside him without being asked.
characters.name: Thomas Raith

characters.description: Michael and Charity Carpenter's eldest child, no longer the kid Harry remembers: a tall teenager in piercings, dye, and deliberate defiance of her mother's household, halfway out the door of her family's faith. Molly calls Harry when her boyfriend is arrested after a bizarre attack at a horror convention, and pulls him into the case. She is smart, stubborn, and carrying more than she tells anyone.
characters.name: Molly Carpenter

characters.description: Karrin Murphy, of Chicago PD's Special Investigations - paying the price for standing with Harry: her unexplained absences have cost her the command of SI, a demotion she absorbs without complaint and without an ounce of regret. Off the org chart or on it, she remains Harry's steadiest ally, and she walks into Faerie itself beside him.
characters.name: Karrin Murphy

characters.description: Michael's wife and the iron center of the Carpenter household, whose long hostility toward Harry has never softened. This case drives into her deepest fears and oldest secrets: Charity was not always a knight's wife baking bread for seven children, and what she gave up - and why - turns out to matter enormously to what is happening now. Her love for her children is a force of nature.
characters.name: Charity Carpenter

characters.description: The shadow of the fallen angel Lasciel, resident in Harry's mind since he grabbed her coin, now an acknowledged - and dangerously useful - presence. She offers recall, translation, tactical help, and access to hellfire that puts real weight behind Harry's magic, all in the tone of a perfectly agreeable companion. Every gift is a payment on the same patient investment: the day Harry digs up the coin.
characters.name: Lasciel's shadow

characters.description: Once a changeling waitress Harry saved from the Stone Table, now the Summer Lady, youngest queen of the Summer Court, with her friend Fix elevated beside her as the Summer Knight. Lily receives Harry with genuine warmth and owes him a debt she has not forgotten, but her office binds what she may say and do; her careful, constrained help - and what she cannot tell him about the strange behavior of Winter - shapes Harry's understanding of the storm gathering between the Courts.
characters.name: Lily

characters.description: The onetime nervous changeling mechanic, now the Summer Knight - the Summer Court's mortal champion, grown into a wiry confidence that sits strangely on Harry's memory of him. Fix serves Lily with total devotion, and like her he is bound in what he may reveal. His transformation since the Stone Table is a quiet reminder of what the Courts' power does to the mortals who take it up.
characters.name: Fix

characters.description: The greatest of the fetches sent out of Winter: a towering shape-shifting horror that wears the forms of fear itself. The Scarecrow leads the phobophages - spirit predators that take the shapes of horror-movie monsters and feed on terror - hunting a specific quarry in Chicago, and it is strong enough to go through a Warden of the White Council to get there. It answers to power deep inside Arctis Tor, Winter's capital.
characters.name: The Scarecrow
